args.content:
module: a1-035
level: A1
sequence: 35
slug: direction-and-origin
version: '2.0'
title: Direction and Origin
subtitle: Where To and Where From
focus: grammar
pedagogy: PPP
phase: A1.4 [Tenses & Daily Life]
word_target: 1200
naturalness:
  status: PASS
  checked_by: Gemini
  date: '2026-03-18'
objectives:
- Express direction using в/у/на + Accusative
- Express direction using до + Genitive
- Express origin using з/від + Genitive
- Distinguish Де?/Куди?/Звідки? question-case pairs
content_outline:
- section: Куди? + Знахідний (Where to? + Accusative)
- section: До + Родовий (To + Genitive)
- section: Звідки? + Родовий (Where from? + Genitive)
- section: Три питання (Three Questions)
- section: Практика (Practice)
vocabulary_hints:
  required:
  - іти
  - їхати
  - до
  - від
  - з/із
  - лікар
  - робота
  - пошта
  - Київ
  - Україна
activity_hints:
- type: fill-in
  items: 12
- type: quiz
  items: 10
- type: match-up
  items: 10
- type: unjumble
  items: 6
connects_to:
- a1-36
prerequisites:
- a1-34
persona:
  voice: Patient Supportive Tutor
  role: Train Conductor
grammar:
- Direction prepositions + Accusative
- До + Genitive
- З/від + Genitive
- Three-question paradigm (Де/Куди/Звідки)
register: розмовний

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

args.content:
- type: fill-in
  id: motion-prepositions
  question: Choose the correct preposition (в/у, на, до, з, від)
  items:
    - label: Я іду ... школу.
      blanks:
        - в
    - label: Він їде ... роботу.
      blanks:
        - на
    - label: Ми йдемо ... лікаря.
      blanks:
        - до
    - label: Вона ... України.
      blanks:
        - з
    - label: Лист ... мами.
      blanks:
        - від
    - label: Я їду ... Київ.
      blanks:
        - у
    - label: Діти йдуть ... пошту.
      blanks:
        - на
    - label: Він виходить ... дому.
      blanks:
        - з
    - label: Вона повертається ...
      blanks:
        - додому
    - label: Я іду ... друга.
      blanks:
        - до
    - label: Книга ... бібліотеки.
      blanks:
        - з
    - label: Подарунок ... тата.
      blanks:
        - від
- type: quiz
  id: de-vs-kudy
  question: Where (Де?) or Where to (Куди?)?
  items:
    - question: "Я ... магазині."
      options: [в, у]
      answer: в
    - question: "Я іду ... магазин."
      options: [в, у]
      answer: в
    - question: "Вона ... роботі."
      options: [на, у]
      answer: на
    - question: "Вона їде ... роботу."
      options: [на, до]
      answer: на
    - question: "Ми ... Києві."
      options: [у, в]
      answer: у
    - question: "Ми їдемо ... Київ."
      options: [у, на]
      answer: у
    - question: "Він ... школі."
      options: [у, в]
      answer: у
    - question: "Він іде ... школу."
      options: [в, на]
      answer: в
    - question: "Де ти? — Я ... пошті."
      options: [на, в]
      answer: на
    - question: "Куди ти йдеш? — Я іду ... пошту."
      options: [на, в]
      answer: на
- type: match-up
  id: question-case-match
  question: Match the question word to its case and typical prepositions.
  items:
    - label: Де?
      value: Locative (в/у, на)
    - label: Куди?
      value: Accusative (в/у, на) or до + Genitive
    - label: Звідки?
      value: Genitive (з/із/зі, від)
    - label: Куди? (to a person)
      value: до + Genitive
    - label: Звідки? (from a place)
      value: з/із/зі + Genitive
    - label: Де? (at work)
      value: на роботі
    - label: Куди? (to work)
      value: на роботу
    - label: Звідки? (from work)
      value: з роботи
    - label: Де? (in the store)
      value: в магазині
    - label: Куди? (to the store)
      value: в магазин
- type: unjumble
  id: sentence-order
  question: Put the words in the correct order.
  items:
    - label: іду / Я / магазин / в
      answer: Я іду в магазин
    - label: їде / Вона / Київ / до
      answer: Вона їде до Києва
    - label: з / Він / України / Одеси
      answer: Він з Одеси
    - label: Подарунок / мами / від
      answer: Подарунок від мами
    - label: повертаюся / Я / додому
      answer: Я повертаюся додому
    - label: лікарню / Вона / в / іде
      answer: Вона іде в лікарню

args.new_string:
| Preposition | When to use | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **з** | before vowels and most consonants | з Одеси, з Києва |
| **із** | between consonants, before sibilants | із Києва, із радістю |
| **зі** | before з-, с-, ш-, щ- and clusters | зі Львова, зі школи |

Don't worry about memorizing every rule — the goal is smooth pronunciation. If a combination feels hard to say, try **із** or **зі** instead.

[!tip] **Quick test**
Say it out loud. If "з" + the next word creates a tongue-twister, switch to **із** or **зі**. Your ear will guide you!

## Три питання (Three Questions)

Three spatial questions form a complete system for talking about location, direction, and origin — like the three stops on every train route.

### The paradigm

| Question | Meaning | Case | Prepositions |
|----------|---------|------|-------------|
| **Де?** | Where? (location) | Locative | в/у, на |
| **Куди?** | Where to? (direction) | Accusative / до + Gen | в/у, на, до |
| **Звідки?** | Where from? (origin) | Genitive | з/із/зі, від |

### Same place, three perspectives

Watch how the same noun changes across all three questions:

**Школа (school):**
- Де? — **В школі.** (at school — Locative)
- Куди? — **В школу.** (to school — Accusative)
- Звідки? — **Зі школи.** (from school — Genitive)

**Робота (work):**
- Де? — **На роботі.** (at work)
- Куди? — **На роботу.** (to work)
- Звідки? — **З роботи.** (from work)

**Київ (Kyiv):**
- Де? — **У Києві.** (in Kyiv)
- Куди? — **У Київ.** / **До Києва.** (to Kyiv)
- Звідки? — **З Києва.** (from Kyiv)

**Лікар (doctor):**
- Де? — **У лікаря.** / **В лікарні.** (at the doctor's / in the hospital)
- Куди? — **До лікаря.** / **В лікарню.** (to the doctor / to the hospital)
- Звідки? — **Від лікаря.** / **З лікарні.** (from the doctor / from the hospital)

[!practice] **Your turn!**
Try building all three forms for **пошта** (post office) and **аеропорт** (airport). Use the patterns above. Check the results: на пошті / на пошту / з пошти; в аеропорту / в аеропорт / з аеропорту.

📺 **Video: ULP 2-47 — Родовий відмінок (Genitive Case)**
This episode of Ukrainian Lessons covers the Genitive case with food and travel examples — a perfect companion to the **до + Родовий** and **Звідки? + Родовий** patterns.
Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhKN_tj05Rg

## Практика (Practice)

Time to put the three questions to work in real conversations.

> [!cultural-note] **Where are you from?**
> In Ukraine, when someone asks **Звідки ви?**, they are often interested in your hometown or region. Local identity is very strong, and people are proud of their origins, whether it's the mountains of the West or the steppes of the South.

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> [!cultural-note] **Returning home**
> The word **додому** is a special adverb that means "towards home". It's one of the first words learners use to describe the end of their day: **Я іду додому**.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Each location can be transformed into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
Answer the question: **Звідки ти?** Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

[!tip] **Quick test**
Say it out loud. If "з" + the next word creates a tongue-twister, switch to **із** or **зі**. Your ear will guide you!

## Три питання (Three Questions)

Three spatial questions form a complete system for talking about location, direction, and origin — like the three stops on every train route.

### The paradigm

| Question | Meaning | Case | Prepositions |
|----------|---------|------|-------------|
...
- Де? — **У лікаря.** / **В лікарні.** (at the doctor's / in the hospital)
- Куди? — **До лікаря.** / **В лікарню.** (to the doctor / to the hospital)
- Звідки? — **Від лікаря.** / **З лікарні.** (from the doctor / from the hospital)

[!practice] **Your turn!**
Try building all three forms for **пошта** (post office) and **аеропорт** (airport). Use the patterns above. Check the results: на пошті / на пошту / з пошти; в аеропорту / в аеропорт / з аеропорту.

📺 **Video: ULP 2-47 — Родовий відмінок (Genitive Case)**
This episode of Ukrainian Lessons covers the Genitive case with food and travel examples — a perfect companion to the **до + Родовий** and **Звідки? + Родовий** patterns.
Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhKN_tj05Rg

## Практика (Practice)

Time to put the three questions to work in real conversations.

> [!cultural-note] **Where are you from?**
> In Ukraine, when someone asks **Звідки ви?**, they are often interested in your hometown or region. Local identity is very strong, and people are proud of their origins, whether it's the mountains of the West or the steppes of the South.

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
...
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> [!cultural-note] **Returning home**
> The word **додому** is a special adverb that means "towards home". It's one of the first words learners use to describe the end of their day: **Я іду додому**.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Each location can be transformed into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
Answer the question: **Звідки ти?** Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

## Activities

(See the activities tab or sidecar file for exercises on direction and origin.)

>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.

> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Practice transforming each location into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
...

resultDisplay.originalContent:
Every journey begins with a question. You already know how to say where things are — **Де?** with the Locative case. Now you're adding two new questions to your toolkit: **Куди?** (where to?) and **Звідки?** (where from?). Think of it like a train conductor's three essential questions: Where is the passenger? Where are they going? Where did they come from?

By the end of this module, you'll be able to tell someone where you're going, how you're getting there, and where you came from — all with the right cases and prepositions.

## Куди? + Знахідний (Where to? + Accusative)

When you want to say where you're going, Ukrainian uses the question word **куди?** (where to?) with the **знахідний відмінок** (Accusative case). You already know the Accusative from earlier modules — now you're using it for direction.

### В/у + Accusative for direction

The prepositions **в** and **у** mean "into" when paired with the Accusative. Notice that masculine inanimate nouns keep their base form — no ending changes:

- Я іду **в магазин**. — I'm going to the store.
- Я їду **у Львів**. — I'm going to Lviv.
- Вона іде **в аеропорт**. — She's going to the airport.
- Ми їдемо **в Україну**. — We're going to Ukraine.

For feminine nouns, the Accusative ending changes just as you learned: **-а → -у**, **-я → -ю**:

- Він іде **в школу**. — He's going to school.
- Я іду **в лікарню**. — I'm going to the hospital.

### На + Accusative for direction

Some places use **на** instead of **в/у** — the same ones that use **на** for location. For direction, **на** also takes the Accusative:

- Я іду **на пошту**. — I'm going to the post office.
- Вона їде **на вокзал**. — She's going to the station.
- Він іде **на роботу**. — He's going to work.

### The key contrast: Де? vs Куди?

This is where learners often mix things up — and where you need to pay extra attention. The same preposition changes meaning depending on the case:

| Question | Case | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| **Де?** (where?) | Locative | в магазин**і** |
| **Куди?** (where to?) | Accusative | в магазин |

- Де ти? — Я **в магазині**. (I'm at the store — Locative)
- Куди ти йдеш? — Я іду **в магазин**. (I'm going to the store — Accusative)

> **(На вулиці / On the street)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш? — Where are you going?
> — Я іду в магазин. — I'm going to the store.
> — А Марія? — And Maria?
> — Вона їде на роботу. — She's going to work.

[!tip] **Іти vs їхати**
Two motion verbs, one rule: **іти** (to go on foot) and **їхати** (to go by vehicle). *Я іду в магазин* — you're walking. *Я їду у Львів* — you're riding (bus, train, car). When in doubt, think: am I walking or riding?

## До + Родового (To + Genitive)

There's another way to say "to" in Ukrainian — the preposition **до**, which always takes the **родового відмінка** (Genitive case). You use **до** when you're heading *towards* someone or something, rather than going *inside*.

### До + Genitive for people and places

- Я іду **до лікаря**. — I'm going to the doctor.
- Вона їде **до Києва**. — She's going to Kyiv.
- Він іде **до одного друга**. — He's going to a friend's.
- Діти йдуть **до школи**. — The children go to school.
<!-- adapted from: Vashulenko, Grade 3 -->

The collocation **додому** (homeward) is written as one word — it's a special adverb:

- Я повертаюся **додому**. — I'm returning home.

### В/у/на vs до: what's the difference?

Both express direction, but with a subtle difference:

- **в/у, на** → you're going *inside* or *onto* the place
- **до** → you're heading *towards* someone or approaching a place

Compare these two sentences:

- Я іду **в лікарню**. — I'm going into the hospital (the building).
- Я іду **до лікаря**. — I'm going to the doctor (the person).

> [!tip] **До with people**
> When you're going to *see someone*, **до** is your go-to preposition: **до лікаря** (to the doctor), **до одного друга** (to a friend), **до мами** (to mom). Think of it as "towards a person."

> [!cultural-note] **Ukrainian hospitality**
> If someone says **ідіть до нас** (come to us), they are likely inviting you for tea or a meal. Ukrainian culture is very social, and people often visit friends "to" their homes.

> [!example] **Quick travel phrase**
> If you are lost and need to get to the center, you can ask: **Як іти до центра?** (How to go to the center?).

> **(Біля дому / Near the house)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш? — Where are you going?
> — Я іду до лікаря. — I'm going to the doctor.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я повертаюся додому. — Then I'm returning home.

## Звідки? + Родового (Where from? + Genitive)

Now for the third question: **Звідки?** (where from?). When you want to say where someone is from or where they're coming from, you use the Genitive case with **з**, **від**, or their variants.

### З/із/зі + Genitive for origin from a place

- Я **з України**. — I'm from Ukraine.
- Він **із Києва**. — He's from Kyiv.
- Вона **зі Львова**. — She's from Lviv.
- Ми **з Одеси**. — We're from Odesa.
- Він виходить **з дому**. — He's going out of the house.

### Від + Genitive for "from a person"

When something comes *from a person*, use **від**:

- **Лист від мами**. — A letter from mom.
- **Подарунок від друга**. — A gift from a friend.

The rule is simple: **з** = from a place (inside), **від** = from a person.

### Euphonic з/із/зі

Ukrainian loves smooth sounds. The preposition **з** has two variants — **із** and **зі** — to avoid awkward consonant clusters:
<!-- adapted from: Litvinova, Grade 5, p. 177 -->

| Preposition | When to use | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **з** | before vowels and most consonants | з Одеси, з Києва |
| **із** | between consonants, before sibilants | із Києва, із радістю |
| **зі** | before з-, с-, ш-, щ- and clusters | зі Львова, зі школи, зі мною |

Don't worry about memorizing every rule — the goal is smooth pronunciation. If a combination feels hard to say, try **із** or **зі** instead.

[!tip] **Quick test**
Say it out loud. If "з" + the next word creates a tongue-twister, switch to **із** or **зі**. Your ear will guide you!

## Три питання (Three Questions)

You now have all three spatial questions. Together, they form a complete system for talking about location, direction, and origin — like the three stops on every train route.

### The paradigm

| Question | Meaning | Case | Prepositions |
|----------|---------|------|-------------|
| **Де?** | Where? (location) | Locative | в/у, на |
| **Куди?** | Where to? (direction) | Accusative / до + Gen | в/у, на, до |
| **Звідки?** | Where from? (origin) | Genitive | з/із/зі, від |

### Same place, three perspectives

Watch how the same noun changes across all three questions:

**Школа (school):**
- Де? — **В школі.** (at school — Locative)
- Куди? — **В школу.** (to school — Accusative)
- Звідки? — **Зі школи.** (from school — Genitive)

**Робота (work):**
- Де? — **На роботі.** (at work)
- Куди? — **На роботу.** (to work)
- Звідки? — **З роботи.** (from work)

**Київ (Kyiv):**
- Де? — **У Києві.** (in Kyiv)
- Куди? — **У Київ.** / **До Києва.** (to Kyiv)
- Звідки? — **З Києва.** (from Kyiv)

**Лікар (doctor):**
- Де? — **У лікаря.** / **В лікарні.** (at the doctor's / in the hospital)
- Куди? — **До лікаря.** / **В лікарню.** (to the doctor / to the hospital)
- Звідки? — **Від лікаря.** / **З лікарні.** (from the doctor / from the hospital)

[!practice] **Your turn!**
Try building all three forms for **пошта** (post office) and **аеропорт** (airport). Use the patterns above as your guide. You can check your answers: на пошті / на пошту / з пошти; в аеропорту / в аеропорт / з аеропорту.

📺 **Video: ULP 2-47 — Родовий відмінок (Genitive Case)**
This episode of Ukrainian Lessons covers the Genitive case with food and travel examples — a perfect companion to the **до + Родовий** and **Звідки? + Родовий** patterns you just learned.
Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhKN_tj05Rg

## Практика (Practice)

Time to put your three questions to work in real conversations.

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Practice transforming each location into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
A friend asks: **Звідки ти?** How do you answer? Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell them where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

# Підсумок
You've just mastered the three essential questions of Ukrainian spatial language. You can now say where you are (**Де?**), where you're going (**Куди?**), and where you're from (**Звідки?**) — each with the right case and prepositions.

Here's what you've learned:
- **Куди?** → Accusative (в/у, на) or **до** + Genitive
- **Звідки?** → **з/із/зі** + Genitive (from a place) or **від** + Genitive (from a person)
- **Іти** (on foot) vs **їхати** (by vehicle)
- The euphonic з/із/зі alternation for smooth pronunciation

**Self-check — can you do these?**

1. Say "I'm going to the post office" in Ukrainian.
2. What's the difference between *Я іду в лікарню* and *Я іду до лікаря*?
3. How do you say "I'm from Kyiv"?
4. When do you use **зі** instead of **з**?

Great work — you've completed the full spatial paradigm! Next up, you'll learn to talk about what happened *yesterday* using the past tense.

[!tip] **Іти vs їхати**
Two motion verbs, one rule: **іти** (to go on foot) and **їхати** (to go by vehicle). *Я іду в магазин* — you're walking. *Я їду у Львів* — you're riding (bus, train, car). When in doubt, think: am I walking or riding?

## До + Родового (To + Genitive)

There's another way to say "to" in Ukrainian — the preposition **до**, which always takes the **родового відмінка** (Genitive case). You use **до** when you're heading *towards* someone or something, rather than going *inside*.

### До + Genitive for people and places

- Я іду **до лікаря**. — I'm going to the doctor.
- Вона їде **до Києва**. — She's going to Kyiv.
- Він іде **до одного друга**. — He's going to a friend's.
- Діти йдуть **до школи**. — The children go to school.
<!-- adapted from: Vashulenko, Grade 3 -->

The collocation **додому** (homeward) is written as one word — it's a special adverb:

- Я повертаюся **додому**. — I'm returning home.

### В/у/на vs до: what's the difference?

Both express direction, but with a subtle difference:

- **в/у, на** → you're going *inside* or *onto* the place
- **до** → you're heading *towards* someone or approaching a place

Compare these two sentences:

- Я іду **в лікарню**. — I'm going into the hospital (the building).
- Я іду **до лікаря**. — I'm going to the doctor (the person).

> [!tip] **До with people**
> When you're going to *see someone*, **до** is your go-to preposition: **до лікаря** (to the doctor), **до одного друга** (to a friend), **до мами** (to mom). Think of it as "towards a person."

> [!cultural-note] **Ukrainian hospitality**
> If someone says **ідіть до нас** (come to us), they are likely inviting you for tea or a meal. Ukrainian culture is very social, and people often visit friends "to" their homes.

> [!example] **Quick travel phrase**
> If you are lost and need to get to the center, you can ask: **Як іти до центра?** (How to go to the center?).

> **(Біля дому / Near the house)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш? — Where are you going?
> — Я іду до лікаря. — I'm going to the doctor.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я повертаюся додому. — Then I'm returning home.

## Звідки? + Родового (Where from? + Genitive)

Now for the third question: **Звідки?** (where from?). When you want to say where someone is from or where they're coming from, you use the Genitive case with **з**, **від**, or their variants.

### З/із/зі + Genitive for origin from a place

- Я **з України**. — I'm from Ukraine.
- Він **із Києва**. — He's from Kyiv.
- Вона **зі Львова**. — She's from Lviv.
- Ми **з Одеси**. — We're from Odesa.
- Він виходить **з дому**. — He's going out of the house.

### Від + Genitive for "from a person"

When something comes *from a person*, use **від**:

- **Лист від мами**. — A letter from mom.
- **Подарунок від друга**. — A gift from a friend.

The rule is simple: **з** = from a place (inside), **від** = from a person.

### Euphonic з/із/зі

Ukrainian loves smooth sounds. The preposition **з** has two variants — **із** and **зі** — to avoid awkward consonant clusters:
<!-- adapted from: Litvinova, Grade 5, p. 177 -->

| Preposition | When to use | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **з** | before vowels and most consonants | з Одеси, з Києва |
| **із** | between consonants, before sibilants | із Києва, із радістю |
| **зі** | before з-, с-, ш-, щ- and clusters | зі Львова, зі школи |

Don't worry about memorizing every rule — the goal is smooth pronunciation. If a combination feels hard to say, try **із** or **зі** instead.

[!tip] **Quick test**
Say it out loud. If "з" + the next word creates a tongue-twister, switch to **із** or **зі**. Your ear will guide you!

## Три питання (Three Questions)

Three spatial questions form a complete system for talking about location, direction, and origin — like the three stops on every train route.

### The paradigm

| Question | Meaning | Case | Prepositions |
|----------|---------|------|-------------|
| **Де?** | Where? (location) | Locative | в/у, на |
| **Куди?** | Where to? (direction) | Accusative / до + Gen | в/у, на, до |
| **Звідки?** | Where from? (origin) | Genitive | з/із/зі, від |

### Same place, three perspectives

Watch how the same noun changes across all three questions:

**Школа (school):**
- Де? — **В школі.** (at school — Locative)
- Куди? — **В школу.** (to school — Accusative)
- Звідки? — **Зі школи.** (from school — Genitive)

**Робота (work):**
- Де? — **На роботі.** (at work)
- Куди? — **На роботу.** (to work)
- Звідки? — **З роботи.** (from work)

**Київ (Kyiv):**
- Де? — **У Києві.** (in Kyiv)
- Куди? — **У Київ.** / **До Києва.** (to Kyiv)
- Звідки? — **З Києва.** (from Kyiv)

**Лікар (doctor):**
- Де? — **У лікаря.** / **В лікарні.** (at the doctor's / in the hospital)
- Куди? — **До лікаря.** / **В лікарню.** (to the doctor / to the hospital)
- Звідки? — **Від лікаря.** / **З лікарні.** (from the doctor / from the hospital)

[!practice] **Your turn!**
Try building all three forms for **пошта** (post office) and **аеропорт** (airport). Use the patterns above. Check the results: на пошті / на пошту / з пошти; в аеропорту / в аеропорт / з аеропорту.

📺 **Video: ULP 2-47 — Родовий відмінок (Genitive Case)**
This episode of Ukrainian Lessons covers the Genitive case with food and travel examples — a perfect companion to the **до + Родовий** and **Звідки? + Родовий** patterns.
Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhKN_tj05Rg

## Практика (Practice)

Time to put the three questions to work in real conversations.

> [!cultural-note] **Where are you from?**
> In Ukraine, when someone asks **Звідки ви?**, they are often interested in your hometown or region. Local identity is very strong, and people are proud of their origins, whether it's the mountains of the West or the steppes of the South.

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> [!cultural-note] **Returning home**
> The word **додому** is a special adverb that means "towards home". It's one of the first words learners use to describe the end of their day: **Я іду додому**.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Each location can be transformed into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
Answer the question: **Звідки ти?** Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

## Activities

(See the activities tab or sidecar file for exercises on direction and origin.)

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Practice transforming each location into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
A friend asks: **Звідки ти?** How do you answer? Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell them where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

# Підсумок
You've just mastered the three essential questions of Ukrainian spatial language. You can now say where you are (**Де?**), where you're going (**Куди?**), and where you're from (**Звідки?**) — each with the right case and prepositions.

Here's what you've learned:
- **Куди?** → Accusative (в/у, на) or **до** + Genitive
- **Звідки?** → **з/із/зі** + Genitive (from a place) or **від** + Genitive (from a person)
- **Іти** (on foot) vs **їхати** (by vehicle)
- The euphonic з/із/зі alternation for smooth pronunciation

**Self-check — can you do these?**

1. Say "I'm going to the post office" in Ukrainian.
2. What's the difference between *Я іду в лікарню* and *Я іду до лікаря*?
3. How do you say "I'm from Kyiv"?
4. When do you use **зі** instead of **з**?

Great work — you've completed the full spatial paradigm! Next up, you'll learn to talk about what happened *yesterday* using the past tense.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**

# Підсумок
You've just mastered the three essential questions of Ukrainian spatial language. You can now say where you are (**Де?**), where you're going (**Куди?**), and where you're from (**Звідки?**) — each with the right case and prepositions.

Here's what you've learned:
- **Куди?** → Accusative (в/у, на) or **до** + Genitive
- **Звідки?** → **з/із/зі** + Genitive (from a place) or **від** + Genitive (from a person)
- **Іти** (on foot) vs **їхати** (by vehicle)
- The euphonic з/із/зі alternation for smooth pronunciation

**Self-check — can you do these?**

1. Say "I'm going to the post office" in Ukrainian.
2. What's the difference between *Я іду в лікарню* and *Я іду до лікаря*?
3. How do you say "I'm from Kyiv"?
4. When do you use **зі** instead of **з**?

resultDisplay.originalContent:
Every journey begins with a question. You already know how to say where things are — **Де?** with the Locative case. Now you're adding two new questions to your toolkit: **Куди?** (where to?) and **Звідки?** (where from?). Think of it like a train conductor's three essential questions: Where is the passenger? Where are they going? Where did they come from?

By the end of this module, you'll be able to tell someone where you're going, how you're getting there, and where you came from — all with the right cases and prepositions.

## Куди? + Знахідний (Where to? + Accusative)

When you want to say where you're going, Ukrainian uses the question word **куди?** (where to?) with the **знахідний відмінок** (Accusative case). You already know the Accusative from earlier modules — now you're using it for direction.

### В/у + Accusative for direction

The prepositions **в** and **у** mean "into" when paired with the Accusative. Notice that masculine inanimate nouns keep their base form — no ending changes:

- Я іду **в магазин**. — I'm going to the store.
- Я їду **у Львів**. — I'm going to Lviv.
- Вона іде **в аеропорт**. — She's going to the airport.
- Ми їдемо **в Україну**. — We're going to Ukraine.

For feminine nouns, the Accusative ending changes just as you learned: **-а → -у**, **-я → -ю**:

- Він іде **в школу**. — He's going to school.
- Я іду **в лікарню**. — I'm going to the hospital.

### На + Accusative for direction

Some places use **на** instead of **в/у** — the same ones that use **на** for location. For direction, **на** also takes the Accusative:

- Я іду **на пошту**. — I'm going to the post office.
- Вона їде **на вокзал**. — She's going to the station.
- Він іде **на роботу**. — He's going to work.

### The key contrast: Де? vs Куди?

This is where learners often mix things up — and where you need to pay extra attention. The same preposition changes meaning depending on the case:

| Question | Case | Example |
|----------|------|---------|
| **Де?** (where?) | Locative | в магазин**і** |
| **Куди?** (where to?) | Accusative | в магазин |

- Де ти? — Я **в магазині**. (I'm at the store — Locative)
- Куди ти йдеш? — Я іду **в магазин**. (I'm going to the store — Accusative)

> **(На вулиці / On the street)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш? — Where are you going?
> — Я іду в магазин. — I'm going to the store.
> — А Марія? — And Maria?
> — Вона їде на роботу. — She's going to work.

[!tip] **Іти vs їхати**
Two motion verbs, one rule: **іти** (to go on foot) and **їхати** (to go by vehicle). *Я іду в магазин* — you're walking. *Я їду у Львів* — you're riding (bus, train, car). When in doubt, think: am I walking or riding?

## До + Родового (To + Genitive)

There's another way to say "to" in Ukrainian — the preposition **до**, which always takes the **родового відмінка** (Genitive case). You use **до** when you're heading *towards* someone or something, rather than going *inside*.

### До + Genitive for people and places

- Я іду **до лікаря**. — I'm going to the doctor.
- Вона їде **до Києва**. — She's going to Kyiv.
- Він іде **до одного друга**. — He's going to a friend's.
- Діти йдуть **до школи**. — The children go to school.
<!-- adapted from: Vashulenko, Grade 3 -->

The collocation **додому** (homeward) is written as one word — it's a special adverb:

- Я повертаюся **додому**. — I'm returning home.

### В/у/на vs до: what's the difference?

Both express direction, but with a subtle difference:

- **в/у, на** → you're going *inside* or *onto* the place
- **до** → you're heading *towards* someone or approaching a place

Compare these two sentences:

- Я іду **в лікарню**. — I'm going into the hospital (the building).
- Я іду **до лікаря**. — I'm going to the doctor (the person).

> [!tip] **До with people**
> When you're going to *see someone*, **до** is your go-to preposition: **до лікаря** (to the doctor), **до одного друга** (to a friend), **до мами** (to mom). Think of it as "towards a person."

> [!cultural-note] **Ukrainian hospitality**
> If someone says **ідіть до нас** (come to us), they are likely inviting you for tea or a meal. Ukrainian culture is very social, and people often visit friends "to" their homes.

> [!example] **Quick travel phrase**
> If you are lost and need to get to the center, you can ask: **Як іти до центра?** (How to go to the center?).

> **(Біля дому / Near the house)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш? — Where are you going?
> — Я іду до лікаря. — I'm going to the doctor.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я повертаюся додому. — Then I'm returning home.

## Звідки? + Родового (Where from? + Genitive)

Now for the third question: **Звідки?** (where from?). When you want to say where someone is from or where they're coming from, you use the Genitive case with **з**, **від**, or their variants.

### З/із/зі + Genitive for origin from a place

- Я **з України**. — I'm from Ukraine.
- Він **із Києва**. — He's from Kyiv.
- Вона **зі Львова**. — She's from Lviv.
- Ми **з Одеси**. — We're from Odesa.
- Він виходить **з дому**. — He's going out of the house.

### Від + Genitive for "from a person"

When something comes *from a person*, use **від**:

- **Лист від мами**. — A letter from mom.
- **Подарунок від друга**. — A gift from a friend.

The rule is simple: **з** = from a place (inside), **від** = from a person.

### Euphonic з/із/зі

Ukrainian loves smooth sounds. The preposition **з** has two variants — **із** and **зі** — to avoid awkward consonant clusters:
<!-- adapted from: Litvinova, Grade 5, p. 177 -->

| Preposition | When to use | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **з** | before vowels and most consonants | з Одеси, з Києва |
| **із** | between consonants, before sibilants | із Києва, із радістю |
| **зі** | before з-, с-, ш-, щ- and clusters | зі Львова, зі школи |

Don't worry about memorizing every rule — the goal is smooth pronunciation. If a combination feels hard to say, try **із** or **зі** instead.

[!tip] **Quick test**
Say it out loud. If "з" + the next word creates a tongue-twister, switch to **із** or **зі**. Your ear will guide you!

## Три питання (Three Questions)

Three spatial questions form a complete system for talking about location, direction, and origin — like the three stops on every train route.

### The paradigm

| Question | Meaning | Case | Prepositions |
|----------|---------|------|-------------|
| **Де?** | Where? (location) | Locative | в/у, на |
| **Куди?** | Where to? (direction) | Accusative / до + Gen | в/у, на, до |
| **Звідки?** | Where from? (origin) | Genitive | з/із/зі, від |

### Same place, three perspectives

Watch how the same noun changes across all three questions:

**Школа (school):**
- Де? — **В школі.** (at school — Locative)
- Куди? — **В школу.** (to school — Accusative)
- Звідки? — **Зі школи.** (from school — Genitive)

**Робота (work):**
- Де? — **На роботі.** (at work)
- Куди? — **На роботу.** (to work)
- Звідки? — **З роботи.** (from work)

**Київ (Kyiv):**
- Де? — **У Києві.** (in Kyiv)
- Куди? — **У Київ.** / **До Києва.** (to Kyiv)
- Звідки? — **З Києва.** (from Kyiv)

**Лікар (doctor):**
- Де? — **У лікаря.** / **В лікарні.** (at the doctor's / in the hospital)
- Куди? — **До лікаря.** / **В лікарню.** (to the doctor / to the hospital)
- Звідки? — **Від лікаря.** / **З лікарні.** (from the doctor / from the hospital)

[!practice] **Your turn!**
Try building all three forms for **пошта** (post office) and **аеропорт** (airport). Use the patterns above. Check the results: на пошті / на пошту / з пошти; в аеропорту / в аеропорт / з аеропорту.

📺 **Video: ULP 2-47 — Родовий відмінок (Genitive Case)**
This episode of Ukrainian Lessons covers the Genitive case with food and travel examples — a perfect companion to the **до + Родовий** and **Звідки? + Родовий** patterns.
Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhKN_tj05Rg

## Практика (Practice)

Time to put the three questions to work in real conversations.

> [!cultural-note] **Where are you from?**
> In Ukraine, when someone asks **Звідки ви?**, they are often interested in your hometown or region. Local identity is very strong, and people are proud of their origins, whether it's the mountains of the West or the steppes of the South.

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> [!cultural-note] **Returning home**
> The word **додому** is a special adverb that means "towards home". It's one of the first words learners use to describe the end of their day: **Я іду додому**.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Each location can be transformed into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
Answer the question: **Звідки ти?** Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

## Activities

- Вона **зі Львова**. — She's from Lviv.
- Ми **з Одеси**. — We're from Odesa.
- Він виходить **з дому**. — He's going out of the house.

### Від + Genitive for "from a person"

When something comes *from a person*, use **від**:

- **Лист від мами**. — A letter from mom.
- **Подарунок від друга**. — A gift from a friend.

The rule is simple: **з** = from a place (inside), **від** = from a person.

### Euphonic з/із/зі

Ukrainian loves smooth sounds. The preposition **з** has two variants — **із** and **зі** — to avoid awkward consonant clusters:
<!-- adapted from: Litvinova, Grade 5, p. 177 -->

| Preposition | When to use | Example |
|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **з** | before vowels and most consonants | з Одеси, з Києва |
| **із** | between consonants, before sibilants | із Києва, із радістю |
| **зі** | before з-, с-, ш-, щ- and clusters | зі Львова, зі школи |

Don't worry about memorizing every rule — the goal is smooth pronunciation. If a combination feels hard to say, try **із** or **зі** instead.

[!tip] **Quick test**
Say it out loud. If "з" + the next word creates a tongue-twister, switch to **із** or **зі**. Your ear will guide you!

## Три питання (Three Questions)

Three spatial questions form a complete system for talking about location, direction, and origin — like the three stops on every train route.

### The paradigm

| Question | Meaning | Case | Prepositions |
|----------|---------|------|-------------|
| **Де?** | Where? (location) | Locative | в/у, на |
| **Куди?** | Where to? (direction) | Accusative / до + Gen | в/у, на, до |
| **Звідки?** | Where from? (origin) | Genitive | з/із/зі, від |

### Same place, three perspectives

Watch how the same noun changes across all three questions:

**Школа (school):**
- Де? — **В школі.** (at school — Locative)
- Куди? — **В школу.** (to school — Accusative)
- Звідки? — **Зі школи.** (from school — Genitive)

**Робота (work):**
- Де? — **На роботі.** (at work)
- Куди? — **На роботу.** (to work)
- Звідки? — **З роботи.** (from work)

**Київ (Kyiv):**
- Де? — **У Києві.** (in Kyiv)
- Куди? — **У Київ.** / **До Києва.** (to Kyiv)
- Звідки? — **З Києва.** (from Kyiv)

**Лікар (doctor):**
- Де? — **У лікаря.** / **В лікарні.** (at the doctor's / in the hospital)
- Куди? — **До лікаря.** / **В лікарню.** (to the doctor / to the hospital)
- Звідки? — **Від лікаря.** / **З лікарні.** (from the doctor / from the hospital)

[!practice] **Your turn!**
Try building all three forms for **пошта** (post office) and **аеропорт** (airport). Use the patterns above. Check the results: на пошті / на пошту / з пошти; в аеропорту / в аеропорт / з аеропорту.

📺 **Video: ULP 2-47 — Родовий відмінок (Genitive Case)**
This episode of Ukrainian Lessons covers the Genitive case with food and travel examples — a perfect companion to the **до + Родовий** and **Звідки? + Родовий** patterns.
Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QhKN_tj05Rg

## Практика (Practice)

Time to put the three questions to work in real conversations.

> [!cultural-note] **Where are you from?**
> In Ukraine, when someone asks **Звідки ви?**, they are often interested in your hometown or region. Local identity is very strong, and people are proud of their origins, whether it's the mountains of the West or the steppes of the South.

> **(На вокзалі / At the train station)**
>
> — Куди ви їдете? — Where are you going?
> — Я їду до Києва. — I'm going to Kyiv.
> — Звідки ви? — Where are you from?
> — Я з Одеси. — I'm from Odesa.

> **(У кафе / At a café)**
>
> — Куди ти йдеш після кави? — Where are you going after coffee?
> — Я іду на пошту. — I'm going to the post office.
> — А потім? — And then?
> — Потім я іду до лікаря. — Then I'm going to the doctor.
> — А звідки ти зараз? — And where are you coming from now?
> — Я з роботи. — I'm from work.

> [!cultural-note] **Returning home**
> The word **додому** is a special adverb that means "towards home". It's one of the first words learners use to describe the end of their day: **Я іду додому**.

> **(Біля аеропорту / Near the airport)**
>
> — Куди вона їде? — Where is she going?
> — Вона їде в аеропорт. — She's going to the airport.
> — А він? — And him?
> — Він повертається додому. — He's returning home.

### Three-question drill

Each location can be transformed into all three forms:

- **Магазин**: у магазині → в магазин → з магазину
- **Школа**: у школі → в школу → зі школи
- **Вокзал**: на вокзалі → на вокзал → з вокзалу
- **Україна**: в Україні → в Україну → з України

[!challenge] **Challenge**
Answer the question: **Звідки ти?** Try: *Я з ...* (your city or country). Then tell where you're going: *Я їду в/до ...*

## Activities

(See the activities tab or sidecar file for exercises on direction and origin.)

# Підсумок
You've just mastered the three essential questions of Ukrainian spatial language. You can now say where you are (**Де?**), where you're going (**Куди?**), and where you're from (**Звідки?**) — each with the right case and prepositions.

Here's what you've learned:
- **Куди?** → Accusative (в/у, на) or **до** + Genitive
- **Звідки?** → **з/із/зі** + Genitive (from a place) or **від** + Genitive (from a person)
- **Іти** (on foot) vs **їхати** (by vehicle)
- The euphonic з/із/зі alternation for smooth pronunciation

**Self-check — can you do these?**

1. Say "I'm going to the post office" in Ukrainian.
2. What's the difference between *Я іду в лікарню* and *Я іду до лікаря*?
3. How do you say "I'm from Kyiv"?
4. When do you use **зі** instead of **з**?

Great work — you've completed the full spatial paradigm! Next up, you'll learn to talk about what happened *yesterday* using the past tense.

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. By default, the tool expects to find and replace exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string`. If you want to replace multiple occurrences of the exact same string, set `allow_multiple` to true. This tool requires providing significant context around the change to ensure precise targeting. Always use the read_file tool to examine the file's current content before attempting a text replacement.
      
      The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.
      
      Expectation for required parameters:
      1. `old_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace (including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.).
      2. `new_string` MUST be the exact literal text to replace `old_string` with (also including all whitespace, indentation, newlines, and surrounding code etc.). Ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic and that `old_string` and `new_string` are different.
      3. `instruction` is the detailed instruction of what needs to be changed. It is important to Make it specific and detailed so developers or large language models can understand what needs to be changed and perform the changes on their own if necessary. 
      4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.
      **Important:** If ANY of the above are not satisfied, the tool will fail. CRITICAL for `old_string`: Must uniquely identify the instance(s) to change. Include at least 3 lines of context BEFORE and AFTER the target text, matching whitespace and indentation precisely. If this string matches multiple locations and `allow_multiple` is not true, the tool will fail.
      5. Prefer to break down complex and long changes into multiple smaller atomic calls to this tool. Always check the content of the file after changes or not finding a string to match.
      **Multiple replacements:** Set `allow_multiple` to true if you want to replace ALL occurrences that match `old_string` exactly.

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

IMPORTANT: The file content has been truncated.
Status: Showing lines 1-100 of 870 total lines.
Action: To read more of the file, you can use the 'start_line' and 'end_line' parameters in a subsequent 'read_file' call. For example, to read the next section of the file, use start_line: 101.

--- FILE CONTENT (truncated) ---
# YAML Activity Format Reference

**Status:** CANONICAL - All levels (A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2)
**Purpose:** Single source of truth for activity YAML schemas
**Last Updated:** January 11, 2026

> ⚠️ **This replaces `ACTIVITY-MARKDOWN-REFERENCE.md`** - All activities MUST be in YAML format.

---

## Critical Rules (READ FIRST)

<critical>

### 1. Root Structure: MUST Be a Bare List

YAML activity files must have a **list at the root level**, NOT wrapped in a dictionary:

```yaml
# ✅ CORRECT - bare list at root
- type: quiz
  title: Quiz title
  items: [...]

- type: match-up
  title: Match title
  pairs: [...]
```

```yaml
# ❌ WRONG - dictionary wrapper (causes schema validation failure)
activities:
  - type: quiz
    title: Quiz title
```

**Why:** The JSON schema validates against a root array. A dictionary wrapper fails validation.

### 2. Property Names Must Match Schema

Use the exact property names defined in `schemas/activities-{level}.schema.json`:

| Activity | Correct Property | Wrong Property |
|----------|-----------------|----------------|
| unjumble | `words` (array) + `answer` | `jumbled`, `scrambled`, `sentence` |
| fill-in | `sentence` | `text`, `prompt` |
| mark-the-words | `text` + `answers` | `passage` + `correct_words` |
| cloze | `passage` | `text` |
| true-false | `statement` | `sentence`, `text` |

### 3. Mark-the-Words: Use `text` + `answers` Array

```yaml
# ✅ CORRECT - plain text with answers array
- type: mark-the-words
  title: Знайдіть іменники
  instruction: Клацніть на всі іменники в реченні.
  text: Гарний день приніс радість у серце.
  answers:
    - день
    - радість
    - серце
```

```yaml
# ❌ WRONG - asterisks in text (was deprecated format)
- type: mark-the-words
  text: Гарний *день* приніс *радість* у *серце*.
  answers: []  # Empty - relies on asterisk extraction
```

**Note:** The asterisk format was used historically but causes issues. Always use explicit `answers` array.

### 4. YAML Quoting for Quoted Text

**Problem:** Statements containing quoted words cause parse errors.

**Examples:**
```yaml
# ❌ WRONG - straight quotes conflict with YAML syntax
statement: Слово "лист" означає email.
# Error: "did not find expected key"

# ✅ CORRECT - Ukrainian guillemets
statement: Слово «лист» означає email.

# ✅ ALSO CORRECT - wrap entire string in single quotes
statement: 'Слово "лист" означає email.'
```

**Recommendation:** Use Ukrainian guillemets («») for quoted text - they're typographically correct and avoid YAML conflicts.

</critical>

---

## Overview

Instead of embedding activities in markdown, we split modules into:

toolCalls.resultDisplay: Read lines 1-100 of 870 from docs/ACTIVITY-YAML-REFERENCE.md
toolCalls.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. If the file is large, the content will be truncated. The tool's response will clearly indicate if truncation has occurred and will provide details on how to read more of the file using the 'start_line' and 'end_line' parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), audio files (MP3, WAV, AIFF, AAC, OGG, FLAC), and PDF files. For text files, it can read specific line ranges.

toolCalls.name: run_shell_command
toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

args.content:
- type: fill-in
  title: Виберіть прийменник (Choose the preposition)
  instruction: Виберіть правильний прийменник для кожного речення.
  items:
    - sentence: Я іду ... школу.
      answer: в
      options: [в, на, до, з]
    - sentence: Він їде ... роботу.
      answer: на
      options: [на, в, до, від]
    - sentence: Ми йдемо ... лікаря.
      answer: до
      options: [до, в, на, з]
    - sentence: Вона ... України.
      answer: з
      options: [з, від, до, в]
    - sentence: Лист ... мами.
      answer: від
      options: [від, з, до, на]
    - sentence: Я їду ... Київ.
      answer: у
      options: [у, на, до, від]
- type: quiz
  title: Де? чи Куди? (Where or Where to?)
  instruction: Виберіть правильну форму залежно від питання.
  items:
    - question: "Де ти? — Я ... магазині."
      options:
        - text: в
          correct: true
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: ув
          correct: false
    - question: "Куди ти йдеш? — Я іду ... магазин."
      options:
        - text: в
          correct: true
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: ув
          correct: false
    - question: "Де вона? — Вона ... роботі."
      options:
        - text: на
          correct: true
        - text: в
          correct: false
        - text: у
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
    - question: "Куди вона їде? — Вона їде ... роботу."
      options:
        - text: на
          correct: true
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: в
          correct: false
        - text: у
          correct: false
    - question: "Де ми? — Ми ... Києві."
      options:
        - text: у
          correct: true
        - text: в
          correct: false
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
    - question: "Куди ми їдемо? — Ми їдемо ... Київ."
      options:
        - text: у
          correct: true
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: в
          correct: false
- type: match-up
  title: Питання та відмінки (Questions and Cases)
  instruction: Поєднайте питання з відповідним відмінком та прийменниками.
  pairs:
    - left: Де?
      right: Locative (в/у, на)
    - left: Куди?
      right: Accusative (в/у, на)
    - left: Звідки?
      right: Genitive (з/із/зі, від)
    - left: Куди? (до людини)
      right: до + Genitive
    - left: Звідки? (від людини)
      right: від + Genitive
    - left: Де? (на роботі)
      right: Locative
- type: unjumble
  title: Складіть речення (Unjumble the sentences)
  instruction: Переставте слова, щоб утворити правильне речення.
  items:
    - words: [Я, іду, в, магазин]
      answer: Я іду в магазин
    - words: [Вона, їде, до, Києва]
      answer: Вона їде до Києва
    - words: [Він, з, Одеси, приїхав]
      answer: Він з Одеси приїхав
    - words: [Подарунок, від, мами, лежить]
      answer: Подарунок від мами лежить
    - words: [Я, повертаюся, додому, сьогодні]
      answer: Я повертаюся додому сьогодні

        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: ув

    - question: "Де вона? — Вона ... роботі."

    - question: "Куди вона їде? — Вона їде ... роботу."
      options:

        - text: в

        - text: у

        - text: до

        - text: в

  title: Питання та відмінки (Questions and Cases)
  instruction: Поєднайте питання з відповідним відмінком та прийменниками.
  pairs:
    - left: Де?
      right: Locative (в/у, на)
    - left: Куди?
      right: Accusative (в/у, на)
    - left: Звідки?
      right: Genitive (з/із/зі, від)
    - left: Куди? (до людини)
      right: до + Genitive
    - left: Звідки? (від людини)
      right: від + Genitive

  title: Складіть речення (Unjumble the sentences)

    - words: [Я, повертаюся, додому, сьогодні]
      answer: Я повертаюся додому сьогодні

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

args.content:
- type: fill-in
  title: Виберіть прийменник (Choose the preposition)
  instruction: Виберіть правильний прийменник для кожного речення.
  items:
    - sentence: Я іду ... школу сьогодні.
      answer: в
      options: [в, на, до, з]
    - sentence: Він їде ... роботу вранці.
      answer: на
      options: [на, в, до, від]
    - sentence: Ми йдемо ... лікаря зараз.
      answer: до
      options: [до, в, на, з]
    - sentence: Вона ... України, з Києва.
      answer: з
      options: [з, від, до, в]
    - sentence: Лист ... мами лежить тут.
      answer: від
      options: [від, з, до, на]
    - sentence: Я їду ... Київ у понеділок.
      answer: у
      options: [у, на, до, від]
- type: quiz
  title: Де? чи Куди? (Where or Where to?)
  instruction: Виберіть правильну форму залежно від питання.
  items:
    - question: "Де ти зараз? — Я ... магазині."
      options:
        - text: в
          correct: true
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: ув
          correct: false
    - question: "Куди ти йдеш? — Я іду ... магазин."
      options:
        - text: в
          correct: true
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: ув
          correct: false
    - question: "Де вона сьогодні? — Вона ... роботі."
      options:
        - text: на
          correct: true
        - text: в
          correct: false
        - text: у
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
    - question: "Куди вона їде? — Вона їде ... роботу."
      options:
        - text: на
          correct: true
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: в
          correct: false
        - text: у
          correct: false
    - question: "Де ми зараз? — Ми ... Києві."
      options:
        - text: у
          correct: true
        - text: в
          correct: false
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
    - question: "Куди ми їдемо? — Ми їдемо ... Київ."
      options:
        - text: у
          correct: true
        - text: на
          correct: false
        - text: до
          correct: false
        - text: в
          correct: false
- type: match-up
  title: Питання та відмінки (Questions and Cases)
  instruction: Поєднайте питання з відповідним відмінком та прийменниками.
  pairs:
    - left: Де?
      right: Locative (в/у, на)
    - left: Куди?
      right: Accusative (в/у, на)
    - left: Звідки?
      right: Genitive (з/із/зі, від)
    - left: Куди? (до людини)
      right: до + Genitive
    - left: Звідки? (від людини)
      right: від + Genitive
    - left: Де? (на роботі)
      right: Locative
- type: unjumble
  title: Складіть речення (Unjumble the sentences)
  instruction: Переставте слова, щоб утворити правильне речення.
  items:
    - words: [Я, іду, в, магазин]
      answer: Я іду в магазин
    - words: [Вона, їде, до, Києва]
      answer: Вона їде до Києва
    - words: [Він, приїхав, з, Одеси]
      answer: Він приїхав з Одеси
    - words: [Подарунок, лежить, від, мами]
      answer: Подарунок лежить від мами
    - words: [Я, повертаюся, додому, сьогодні]
      answer: Я повертаюся додому сьогодні
    - words: [Вона, іде, в, лікарню]
      answer: Вона іде в лікарню
